items.definition_en: A cart used to carry luggage at airports or stations.
items.example_en: She loaded her suitcases onto a baggage trolley.

items.definition_en: A place where passports are checked at borders or airports.
items.example_en: They waited in line at passport control.

items.definition_en: The area where people wait for passengers who have just arrived.
items.example_en: We met them in the arrivals hall.

items.definition_en: A sign showing times and gates for upcoming departures.
items.example_en: Check the departure board for your flight details.

items.definition_en: A moving belt that delivers baggage to passengers.
items.example_en: Our bags came out on the luggage belt.

items.definition_en: A gate at a station where you must show your ticket to pass.
items.example_en: Scan your ticket at the ticket barrier.

items.definition_en: An office for items that have been found in public places.
items.example_en: I went to the lost property office for my umbrella.

items.definition_en: A comfortable waiting area for first class passengers.
items.example_en: She relaxed in the first class lounge before her flight.

items.definition_en: A door that opens automatically when you approach.
items.example_en: He walked through the automatic door at the entrance.

items.definition_en: A schedule of trains’ departure and arrival times.
items.example_en: Check the train timetable for the next departure.

items.definition_en: The number identifying which platform a train leaves from.
items.example_en: The platform number is printed on your ticket.

items.definition_en: A storage area above passengers’ seats for baggage.
items.example_en: Put your carry-on bag in the overhead compartment.

items.definition_en: A line of people waiting for their turn.
items.example_en: We stood in the waiting line for security.

items.definition_en: An area for loading and unloading passengers or baggage.
items.example_en: The taxi stopped in the loading zone.

items.definition_en: A machine where passengers can check themselves in.
items.example_en: He uses the self check-in kiosk to print his boarding pass.

items.definition_en: A bus that transports people between the airport and other locations.
items.example_en: We took the airport shuttle to the hotel.

items.definition_en: A tunnel that allows people to cross under railway tracks.
items.example_en: Please use the railway underpass to reach platform two.

items.definition_en: A person who checks tickets and helps passengers on a train.
items.example_en: The train conductor checked everyone's tickets.

items.definition_en: An electronic display showing the status of flights.
items.example_en: Look at the flight information screen for updates.

items.definition_en: A covered area on a platform to protect passengers from weather.
items.example_en: We waited for the train under the platform shelter.
